text: الْمُكَاتَبُ لَا يَجُوزُ لَهُ عِتْقٌ وَ لَا هِبَةٌ وَ لَا نِكَاحٌ وَ لَا شَهَادَةٌ وَ لَا حَجٌّ حَتَّى يُؤَدِّيَ جَمِيعَ مَا عَلَيْهِ إِذَا كَانَ مَوْلَاهُ قَدْ شَرَطَ عَلَيْهِ إِنْ هُوَ عَجَزَ عَنْ نَجْمٍ مِنْ نُجُومِهِ فَهُوَ رَدٌّ فِي الرِّقِّ .

en.hubeali: Ibn Mahboub, from Ali Bin Abu Hamza, from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The contracted slave, it is not allowed for him an emancipation, nor a gifting, nor marriage, nor testimony, nor a Hajj, until he pays off the entirety of what is upon him, when his master that stipulated upon him that if he was frustrated (from paying off), from an installment from his installments, so he would be returned into the slavery’.
